mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.4pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts amplifies aggregate demand growth, which compresses equity risk premiums and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to generate a positive mean forward return over 60 days, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of low monetary policy rates indicates accommodative financing for corporate output expansion, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The positive return signal vanishes when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.5%, or INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns in low-rate environments with Rank IC below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding corporate revenue capacity and wage-driven consumption, which systematically generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward returns across multiple rolling 2-year windows, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises discount rates and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.4% threshold, invalidating the tightening fear channel.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling real personal consumption expenditures indicates corporate profit margin compression from weak consumer demand, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The negative return signal disappears when GDPC1 and RSAFS move in opposite directions, or the interaction effect yields a Rank IC below 0.02 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during periods of high unemployment signals a severe demand-supply mismatch that forces corporate earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns when UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the mechanism shows no predictive power in recessionary periods with t-stat below 1.5.
